propositions: rebar-mcp is currently stdio-only with no HTTP transport, no network bind, and no authentication.
Add an optional Streamable-HTTP transport that acts as an OAuth 2.1 Resource Server validating inbound bearer tokens and advertising RFC 9728 Protected Resource Metadata.
Ship five provider-agnostic token verifiers (static bearer, JWKS/JWT, RFC 7662 introspection, trusted-proxy passthrough, pluggable custom) selectable via [mcp.auth] config and REBAR_MCP_* env vars.
Every verifier must validate token audience/resource itself since the SDK does not, and must fail closed on any error.
An unauthenticated request must be rejected with 401 plus a WWW-Authenticate header carrying the resource_metadata URL; insufficient scope yields 403 and wrong audience is rejected.
Starting an HTTP transport with auth disabled must fail fast unless REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_UNAUTHENTICATED_HTTP is explicitly set, with stdio remaining the default and auth off by default.
